footer.description: Transforming Alto Hospicio's textile landfill into a circular economy model for the Atacama Desert.

hero.subtitle: Renace Atacama is an initiative that combines sustainability, innovation and employment. We turn waste into new products, strengthening communities and caring for the planet.
hero.alt: Renace Atacama — recycling with real impact

ambiental.title: Environmental impacts
ambiental.text: Soil and water pollution, ecosystem degradation and air pollution.

social.title: Social impacts
social.text: Health risks and deepening inequality and social conflict.

economico.title: Economic impacts
economico.text: Pollution affects the tourism industry, agriculture and fishing in the region.

vertederos.title: The landfills of Alto Hospicio
vertederos.text: Alto Hospicio and its surroundings receive daily tons of industrial and urban waste (clothing, tires, bottles and more), generating illegal dumps in the middle of the desert. With more than 300 hectares affected and nearly 39,000 tons per year, they have become one of the major environmental crises in Tarapacá, with social, economic and ecological impacts.

accionSocial.title: Renace Atacama is rebuilding life and hope from the community.
accionSocial.subtitle: We work with communities, women, recyclers and youth to strengthen the solidarity economy, participation and dignity in the territory.
accionSocial.card1Title: Heal the desert, heal its people
accionSocial.card1Text: At Renace Atacama we believe that healing the desert is also healing its people.
accionSocial.card2Title: A crisis that demands immediate action
accionSocial.card2Text: The scale of the problem directly affects communities, public health, the landscape and productive activities in the area.

escarabajosSection.title: Desert Beetles
escarabajosSection.intro1: The desert cleanup work inspired us in the dung beetle: a key organism that recycles nutrients, disperses seeds and acts as a bioindicator of ecosystem health.
escarabajosSection.intro2: It will be the symbol of the project and an invitation to join.
escarabajosSection.ambassadorDesc: Spread the project in your community.

cta.headline: Ready to join? Together we regenerate the Atacama Desert.

contacto.title: Contact us
contacto.subtitle: If you want to join, collaborate or promote partnerships with Renace Atacama, let's talk.
contacto.ctaTitle: Together we regenerate the Atacama Desert.
contacto.ctaSub: Your voice and your action matter.

nosotros.tagline: Regeneration, circularity and community at the center of every decision.
nosotros.title: Who we are
nosotros.introBefore: Renace Atacama is an initiative born from
nosotros.introAfter: , a platform of women that promotes inclusion, circularity and collaboration as engines of transformation. We seek to create an innovation hub in the Atacama Desert by putting communities at the center, creating opportunities for the territory and generating solutions to the problems associated with waste.
nosotros.beginssCard: The project is driven by an interdisciplinary team of experts and local actors, integrating sustainability, innovation and community action to restore the territory and activate new circular economies.
nosotros.teamTitle: Phase 1 Diagnostic and Master Plan Team
nosotros.teamSub: A group of professionals that combines sustainability, industry, community, communication and territory.
nosotros.partnersSub: Renace Atacama is driven by organizations and institutions committed to sustainability.
nosotros.sponsorsSub: Public entities that support and sponsor Renace Atacama.
nosotros.collaboratorsTitle: Professionals who collaborate with Renace Atacama in different initiatives

elProblema.title: The textile wasteland of the desert
elProblema.subtitle: Illegal landfills in Alto Hospicio have become one of the largest sources of pollution in northern Chile, affecting biodiversity, public health and the regional economy.

ambiental.title: Environmental impact
ambiental.text: Soil, air and water pollution. Damage to the flora and fauna of the Atacama Desert.

social.title: Social impact
social.text: Direct impact on nearby communities, health risks and deterioration in quality of life.

economico.title: Economic impact
economico.text: Cleanup costs, loss of territorial value and pressure on public resources.

elProyecto.title: The Renace Atacama Project
elProyecto.subtitle: Renace Atacama seeks to transform Alto Hospicio and the Atacama desert into a hub of innovation, regeneration and sustainability.
elProyecto.problemText: Illegal landfills in Alto Hospicio have become one of the largest sources of pollution in northern Chile, affecting biodiversity, public health and the regional economy.
elProyecto.projectTitle: Industrial park and community technological development and innovation center
elProyecto.economiaText: We transform illegal landfills into opportunities for economic and territorial development.
elProyecto.nodoText: An industrial park open to the community that integrates recycling, education, innovation and local development.
elProyecto.modeloText: A model that transforms waste into resources, drives innovation and generates local employment.
elProyecto.modalidad1: The industrial park and community technological development and innovation center will be the operational heart of Renace Atacama. Located near the Alto Hospicio landfills, its function is to give a new destination to the waste that currently affects the territory, starting with textiles and progressively expanding to other materials according to local needs.
elProyecto.modalidad2: More than a recycling plant, the center is conceived as a meeting and learning space open to the community. There innovation, environmental education, technical training and applied research will converge, fostering new local economies and collaborative work among professionals, organizations and inhabitants of the territory.
elProyecto.modalidad3: The goal is to generate a self-sustaining model where material recovery drives the creation of social and economic value, consolidating Alto Hospicio as a benchmark in sustainable management and environmental regeneration in northern Chile.
elProyecto.ejeAmbientalDesc: Conservation and responsible use of resources; ecological restoration; sustainable balance between human activity and the environment.
elProyecto.ejeSocialDesc: Equity, access to basic services, health and safety, strengthening, relationship and protection of vulnerable groups.
elProyecto.ejeComunitarioDesc: Participation of stakeholders; local governance; culture and heritage; collaborative projects.
elProyecto.ejeEconomicoDesc: Sustainable and inclusive growth; local employment; circular value chains; clean technologies and energy.
elProyecto.ejesSectionTitle: Lines of action of the proposal
elProyecto.ejesSectionSub: Renace Atacama emerges as a transformative response to the challenges of the territory. It turns waste into opportunities through a sustainable, innovative and long-term approach, integrating different disciplines and placing women's empowerment at the center. All of this is articulated under a circular economy model oriented towards sustainable development.

resultados.transformarText: Collectively transform a damaged territory into a healthy and resilient space, where collaboration between communities generates lasting ties, exchange of knowledge and a deep commitment to prevent these wounds from reopening.
resultados.crearText: Drive creative productive chains, based on observation, experience, applied learning and imagination, fostering the co-creation of sustainable solutions that give rise to a new way of life in harmony with the environment.
resultados.restaurarText: Respectfully remove all waste and materials foreign to the ecosystem, recognizing the unique and fragile value of the Atacama Desert, and acting with responsibility towards its preservation.
resultados.conectarText: Integrate into global environmental collaboration networks, such as the sustainable fashion movement, sharing good practices and experiences through learning by doing, and contributing concrete examples of regeneration, creativity and ecological awareness.

laPropuesta.title: The proposal
laPropuesta.subtitle: Renace Atacama responds to the challenges of the territory by transforming waste into opportunities. A circular action model based on sustainability, innovation and community.
laPropuesta.expectedResultsSub: A clear plan to transform the problem into a sustainable, replicable system with real impact.

resultados.transformarText: Waste into resources, with initial focus on textiles.
resultados.crearText: Local employment, training and circular entrepreneurship.
resultados.conectarText: Partnerships between community, industry and institutions.

productos.title: What can be obtained from recycled clothing?
productos.subtitle: A wide variety of useful, creative and sustainable products that give new life to recovered textiles.
cat7Items: Fibers mixed with recycled for panels

quienesSomos.title: Who we are
quienesSomos.subtitle: Renace Atacama is an initiative driven by Begins, focused on innovation, sustainability and circular economy to turn waste into opportunities and regenerate the territory together with its communities.
quienesSomos.visionText: Restore the Atacama Desert and activate circular economies with social, environmental and productive impact.

accionSocial.title: Social and community action
accionSocial.subtitle: At Renace Atacama we believe that healing the desert is also healing its people.
accionSocial.text: We work with communities, women, recyclers and youth to strengthen the solidarity economy, participation and dignity in the territory.
accionSocial.quote: Renace Atacama is rebuilding life and hope from the community.

galeria.title: Gallery
galeria.subtitle: Images from the field that show the problem of the landfills.

contenido.title: The documentary
contenido.subtitle: The project includes the production of two feature-length documentaries that will show the problem in the region and the process of creating the innovation hub and desert cleanup. Documentaries and visual gallery that account for the work in the field.
contenido.twoDocsSub: Feature films that will spread the problem, causes and proposed solutions, with international reach and community focus. Content for campaigns and mobilization, positioning Renace Atacama as a benchmark in innovation and sustainability.
contenido.doc1Text: Aimed at streaming platforms, it will spread the problem, causes and proposed solutions with international reach.
contenido.doc2Text: In collaboration with our partner channel, it will follow the experience in the field, community organization and the real impact of the actions.
contenido.doc3Text: Content for campaigns and mobilization, positioning Renace Atacama as a benchmark in innovation and sustainability.
contenido.galleryAlt2: Land and landfills in Alto Hospicio
contenido.gallerySub: Images from the field that show the problem of the landfills and the regeneration work.

escarabajos.title: Desert Beetles
escarabajos.subtitle: The desert cleanup work inspired us in the dung beetle: a key organism that recycles nutrients, disperses seeds and acts as a bioindicator of ecosystem health.
escarabajos.symbolQuote: It will be the symbol of the project and an invitation to join.
escarabajos.symbolDesc: The beetle represents regeneration, resource recycling and community work in the territory.
escarabajos.ambassadorDesc: Spread the project in your community.
escarabajos.ctaHeadline: Ready to join? Together we regenerate the Atacama Desert.

productsSection.intro: We turn textile waste and tires into industrial and textile lines that return value to the territory.
productsSection.industrialDesc: Recycled materials for insulation, technical felts and components that plug into new production chains.
productsSection.textileDesc: Ponchos, jackets, bags and accessories made from recovered textiles and local craft.
productsSection.alliesDesc: Products designed to join production chains and projects with territorial impact.

tires.title: Products from recycled tires
tires.desc: We turn recovered rubber from end-of-life tires into durable pieces for public spaces, industry, and homes—closing the loop on hard-to-dispose materials.

shoes.title: Soles and footwear from recovered materials
shoes.desc: We blend recovered rubber and fibers for hard-wearing soles with solid grip and a smaller environmental footprint—work, sport, and everyday wear.

compost.title: Compost and organic recovery
compost.desc: Sustainable organic-waste management solutions in Alto Hospicio and Iquique.
compost.subtitle: What we do
compost.detail: We provide sustainable organic-waste management solutions in the municipalities of Alto Hospicio and Iquique. We promote incorporating composting into everyday life in a simple and accessible way for residents of the Atacama Desert, fostering a culture of greater responsibility and environmental awareness across the territory.
compost.body: Detailed content will be published when the line is available.

b2b.title: Industrial line (B2B)
b2b.desc: Mainly for retail, automotive industry, mattress industry, major brands, and sneaker soles.

b2c.title: Products made from recycled textiles
b2c.desc: Sold online (Escarabajos Store) under the Escarabajos del Desierto brand.

jointVenture.title: Joint Venture
jointVenture.subtitle: Renace Atacama is being developed by a Joint Venture between
jointVenture.subtitle2: , who are responsible for the structuring, implementation, and expansion of the project.
jointVenture.Twosubtitle: This alliance combines technical capability, strategic vision, and on-the-ground execution, guaranteeing robustness and consistency in generating environmental, social, and economic impact in the region.
jointVenture.doc1Text: Brings its expertise in the development of environmental assets, including carbon and plastic credits, participating in every stage of the process: from conception and structuring to registration, monitoring, management, and marketing.
jointVenture.doc1Text2: In addition, it contributes to ESG strategies, emissions inventories, and carbon neutralization processes, thereby strengthening the project’s technical foundation and credibility.
jointVenture.doc2Text: Complements the organization with its vision and ability to execute, playing a direct role in implementing initiatives in the region.
jointVenture.doc2Text2: Its collaboration with OMA enables the transformation of strategy into action, ensuring consistency and alignment with the local context.

bioOma.title: Bio OMA
bioOma.subtitle: Using a systemic approach,
bioOma.subtitle2: develops projects involving carbon credits, plastic credits, and other environmental assets within the framework of the circular economy for waste, participating in all stages of project development, from asset generation to the implementation of socio-environmental practices. These stages range from conception and structuring, registration, and monitoring to the management and marketing of assets, offering a comprehensive professional service so that our clients can add value to their products and services in a sustainable manner.
bioOma.Twosubtitle: has extensive experience in sustainability consulting, including the development of corporate ESG strategies, the creation of performance metrics, and the structuring of reports, as well as emissions inventories and the offsetting of greenhouse gas emissions.
bioOma.SubtFounder: + 20 years of global experience in sustainability. Has been working with carbon credits since 2004
bioOma.DescFounder: Throughout her 20-year international career, Juliana has established herself in the market as a sustainability and territorial development strategist, designing and developing business models capable of generating value for stakeholders. Her core philosophy is a systemic approach to sustainability linked to innovation and smart financial mechanisms. She gained her first experience in the carbon market in 2004 in Europe—where she lived for over a decade—during the first phase of the Kyoto Protocol, and since then has been developing projects and trading carbon credits and other environmental assets. A graduate in International Trade, she completed various sustainability courses in Europe, earned an MBA in Corporate Finance, and is currently pursuing a Ph.D. in Environmental Engineering at the University of Rome – Tor Vergata. In 2009, she founded Eco Business Solutions. Drawing on her extensive experience as Eco, she structured a new business model and led a fundraising round to launch OMA ACTIVOS AMBIENTALES S.A., of which she is co-founder and CEO. She is a volunteer director at Pro Natura International, a member of the European Technical and Scientific Committee of Remtech Europe, and has worked and lived in various countries, including Italy, France, Spain, Germany, Poland, Egypt, Mexico, and the U.S., among four others.

bioBeginns.title: Bio Beginss
bioBeginns.subtitle: is a women-led platform that promotes inclusion, circularity, and collaboration as drivers of transformation. We aim to create an innovation hub in the Atacama Desert by putting communities at the center, creating opportunities for the region, and developing solutions to waste-related challenges.

proyectosComunitarios.title: Community Projects
proyectosComunitarios.subtitle: The proposed community projects develop sustainable solutions that integrate environmental education, the circular economy, and local development.
proyectosComunitarios.project1Desc: Biocomposting transforms organic waste into compost, promoting responsible practices and reducing environmental impact.
proyectosComunitarios.project2Desc: Reforestation promotes the recovery of degraded ecosystems, enhancing biodiversity and community involvement.
proyectosComunitarios.project3Desc: The clothing tourism route promotes the reuse of textiles, raising awareness and creating new economic opportunities.
proyectosComunitarios.text: Together, these projects offer educational and hands-on experiences that foster more resilient communities committed to their surroundings.
